Grammar · 10 min
Simple Present & Never Ever
Click examples to see the translation

Simple Present — Structure

Subject
+
Verb (base)
+
Time expression
I wake up at 6 a.m. every day.
Me despierto a las 6 a.m. todos los días.
She exercises in the morning.
Ella hace ejercicio por la mañana.
They hang out on weekends.
Ellos salen juntos los fines de semana.
He cooks dinner at night.
Él cocina la cena por la noche.

Never Ever — Emphasis

🚫 Never Ever

Use "never ever" to emphasize that something absolutely never happens. It is stronger than just "never."

I never ever wake up before 7 a.m.
Jamás me despierto antes de las 7 a.m.
She never ever goes to bed before midnight.
Ella jamás se va a dormir antes de la medianoche.
I never ever skip breakfast.
Jamás me salto el desayuno.
Grammar Practice · 15 min
Truth and a Lie
Two statements — one is true, one is a lie. Can you guess which?
Each person shares 2 sentences about their daily routine using Simple Present. One is TRUE, one is a LIE. The class guesses which is which!
Round 1 of 6
Statement A
✅ Truth
Statement B
🚫 Lie
1 / 6
Speaking Activity · 10 min
Routine Chain Reaction
Build the longest routine chain without breaking it!
Each student adds one activity to the chain using Simple Present. You must repeat all previous activities before adding yours. If you forget — the chain breaks! 💥
Time per turn
5
Chain length: 0
Chain starts here...
